name: 40_entity_resolution
description: Alias merging (5 refs -> 1 node), distinct-person separation (2 Sarahs -> 2 nodes), role change invalidation

steps.user_message: Sarah joined our team this year as a developer.
Sarah Chen shipped the sync feature ahead of schedule.
Our lead dev rewrote the storage layer over the weekend.
steps.assistant_response: Impressive turnaround by your lead dev.

steps.user_message: The Google hire keeps raising the bar — she refactored the whole memory engine.
steps.assistant_response: The ex-Google experience is clearly paying off.

steps.user_message: Sarah Chen, our lead developer who came from Google, is presenting at the team offsite.
steps.assistant_response: Noted — Sarah Chen presenting at the offsite.

steps.label: one Sarah Chen node, not five
steps.query: MATCH (e:Entity) WHERE toLower(e.name) CONTAINS 'sarah' RETURN e.name

steps.label: alias facts converge: lead-dev query surfaces Sarah Chen

steps.user_message: Separately — Sarah Kim from our external accounting firm needs the receipts by Friday for the BAS.
steps.assistant_response: Sarah Kim (accounting) needs receipts by Friday.

steps.label: two DIFFERENT Sarahs are two nodes
steps.query: MATCH (e:Entity) WHERE toLower(e.name) CONTAINS 'sarah' RETURN e.name

steps.label: accountant Sarah doesn't absorb dev facts

steps.user_message: Big news: I promoted Sarah Chen to CTO. She's no longer just lead developer.
steps.assistant_response: Sarah Chen is now CTO — congratulations to her.

steps.label: current role (CTO) is the top answer
role-change did not spawn a duplicate Sarah Chen node
steps.query: MATCH (e:Entity) WHERE toLower(e.name) CONTAINS 'sarah' AND NOT toLower(e.name) CONTAINS 'kim' RETURN e.name

steps.user_message: Also, we renamed the company — IIMAGINE is now trading as Cortexa.

steps.label: rename links old and new names
steps.query: what is the company called now
